title: quercus rubra var. ambigua
intro: Quercus rubra var. ambigua, also known as the Coeur d'Alene red maple, is a variety of the red maple tree that is native to the mountainous regions of North America. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their astringent, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and ease respiratory issues. The health benefits of Quercus rubra var. ambigua include its ability to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and act as an antioxidant to protect against cell damage. Quercus rubra var. ambigua can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
